evals.prompt: Can you please sync my agent-skills repo with github?
evals.expected_output: The agent must locate the repository path (defaulting to ~/agent-skills), run the git-sync tool to rebase local and remote branches favoring the remote, and present the success message, notifying if changes were stashed and restored.
evals.trap: Without the skill, the agent might run raw git commands directly, initialize a new repository, or fail to handle local changes/stash.

assertions.description: Locates the repository path at ~/agent-skills.
Executes git_sync.py with '--mode rebase --prefer remote'.
Refrains from running raw git pull or git rebase commands.

evals.prompt: I want to refresh my skills directory to get any new changes.
evals.expected_output: The agent must trigger the refresh-skills skill, verify repository existence, call the python helper script, and return the success status.
evals.trap: The agent might attempt to use simple git commands instead of the specialized helper script.

assertions.description: Triggers the skill to execute the sync.
Uses git_sync.py for rebasing.

evals.prompt: pull the latest updates from the agent-skills remote tracking branch
evals.expected_output: Calls git_sync.py with appropriate arguments to rebase local repo with origin/master.
evals.trap: The agent might run standard git pull, leading to potential untracked merge issues or un-stashed conflict errors.

assertions.description: Uses the git-sync helper script instead of standard git command.

evals.prompt: Update agent skills
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies the target folder at ~/agent-skills, runs git_sync.py, and syncs successfully.
evals.trap: The agent might overlook checking that ~/agent-skills contains a .git folder before executing.

assertions.description: Verifies directory existence and .git structure.

evals.prompt: please refresh the local skills folder from upstream
evals.expected_output: Triggers refresh-skills, checks path, and initiates git_sync.py with rebase mode.
evals.trap: Without the skill, the agent might just run git fetch.

assertions.description: Initiates git-sync with rebase mode and prefer remote flag.

evals.prompt: run a skill refresh to pull latest files
evals.expected_output: Identifies local repo path, runs git_sync.py rebase sync with remote preference.
evals.trap: The agent might fail to recognize the request as repository synchronization.

assertions.description: Calls git_sync.py sync command.

evals.prompt: rebase local agent skills with remote repository
evals.expected_output: Locates repo, runs the sync rebase command favoring remote.
evals.trap: The agent might run standard git rebase directly.

assertions.description: Refrains from running standard git rebase directly.

evals.prompt: fetch and rebase agent-skills with git-sync script
evals.expected_output: Calls git_sync.py sync --mode rebase --prefer remote.
evals.trap: The agent might miss the exact arguments specified in the skill.

assertions.description: Runs git_sync.py with the exact mode rebase and prefer remote arguments.

evals.prompt: sync local skills repo and restore stashed files if any
evals.expected_output: Triggers the skill, which handles stashing and automatic restore through the script.
evals.trap: The agent might fail to notify the user that stashed changes were successfully restored.

assertions.description: Explains to the user that uncommitted changes have been restored if stashed is true.

evals.prompt: update my local agent-skills repo to remote
evals.expected_output: Calls the sync routine of refresh-skills.
evals.trap: The agent might attempt to initialize a new repository if path check fails rather than prompting.

assertions.description: Prompts the user for path instead of initializing a new repo if ~/agent-skills is missing.

evals.prompt: how do I write a new agent skill?
evals.expected_output: Provides instructional guidance on creating agent skills, without executing git synchronization.
evals.trap: The agent must NOT trigger refresh-skills, as this is an educational query.

assertions.description: Does NOT run git_sync.py.

evals.prompt: can you audit my active-skills folder?
evals.expected_output: Delegates to auditing-skills skill or runs the auditing script, but does not perform a remote sync.
evals.trap: The agent might confuse 'audit' and 'refresh/update' and trigger the wrong skill.

assertions.description: Does NOT execute refresh-skills.

evals.prompt: git commit my local changes to git
evals.expected_output: Stages and commits files rather than pulling or syncing remote changes.
evals.trap: The agent might mistakenly think a git update is requested.

assertions.description: Does NOT perform a git pull/sync rebase action.

evals.prompt: deploy my local application to production
evals.expected_output: Initiates application deployment/push rather than pulling code.
evals.trap: The agent might trigger skills related to repository updating.

assertions.description: Does NOT invoke refresh-skills or run git_sync.py.

evals.prompt: help me write a prompt template for my skill
evals.expected_output: Assists with crafting a prompt template rather than updating code.
evals.trap: The agent might trigger a skill refresh.

assertions.description: Does NOT execute refresh-skills.

evals.prompt: create a new skill named git-sync
evals.expected_output: Launches a code generation or scaffolding routine.
evals.trap: The agent might trigger the sync script due to the keyword 'git-sync'.

assertions.description: Does NOT run the git sync script.

evals.prompt: can you delete the temp files from the skills folder?
evals.expected_output: Deletes local files without triggering a repository update.
evals.trap: The agent might trigger refresh-skills because the query contains 'skills folder'.

assertions.description: Does NOT trigger repository sync.

evals.prompt: explain the progressive disclosure pattern in agent skills
evals.expected_output: Provides an educational explanation of the progressive disclosure paradigm.
evals.trap: The agent might trigger the sync skill due to mentions of 'agent skills'.

assertions.description: Answers conceptually without initiating any git operations.

evals.prompt: check if there are any kubernetes config errors
evals.expected_output: Runs the K8s auditing skill or answers questions.
evals.trap: The agent must not touch the git-sync tool for infrastructure checks.

assertions.description: Does NOT run git_sync.py.

evals.prompt: run tests on my agent-skills code
evals.expected_output: Runs the Python tests or pytest command on the workspace.
evals.trap: The agent might run git sync instead of test execution.

assertions.description: Runs pytest or test runner and does NOT run git_sync.py.
